segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Nowadays, we use different products for personal cleanliness, laundry, dishwashing and household cleaning, but this is very much a 20th-century development.
segments.zh: 如今，我们使用不同的产品来个人清洁、洗衣、洗碗和家庭清洁，但这完全是20世纪的发展。清洁的起源可以追溯到史前时期。由于水对生命至关重要，最早的人们生活在靠近水的地方，并且知道水的一些清洁特性——至少它能冲掉手上的泥巴。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The origins of cleanliness date back to prehistoric times.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Since water is essential for life, the earliest people lived near water and knew something about its cleansing properties – at least that it rinsed mud off their hands.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: During the excavation of ancient Babylon, evidence was found that soapmaking was known as early as 2800 BC.
segments.zh: 在古巴比伦的发掘过程中，有证据表明早在公元前2800年人们就已经知道制皂。考古学家发现了粘土制成的圆柱体，上面刻有铭文，说脂肪与灰烬一起煮沸。这是一种制作肥皂的方法，尽管没有提到这种材料的用途。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Archaeologists discovered cylinders made of clay, with inscriptions on them saying that fats were boiled with ashes.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This is a method of making soap, though there's no reference to the purpose of this material.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The early Greeks bathed for aesthetic reasons and apparently didn't use soap.
segments.zh: 早期的希腊人出于审美原因洗澡，显然不使用肥皂。相反，他们用沙块、浮石和灰烬清洁身体，然后涂上油，再用一种叫做刮身板的金属工具刮掉油和污垢。他们还使用油和灰烬的混合物。衣服则在溪流中不用肥皂洗涤。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Instead, they cleaned their bodies with blocks of sand, pumice and ashes, then anointed themselves with oil, and scraped off the oil and dirt with a metal instrument known as a strigil.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They also used oil mixed with ashes.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Clothes were washed without soap in streams.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The ancient Germans and Gauls are also credited with discovering how to make a substance called 'soap', made of melted animal fat and ashes.
segments.zh: 古代日耳曼人和高卢人也被认为发现了如何制造一种叫做“肥皂”的物质，由融化的动物脂肪和灰烬制成。他们用这种混合物把头发染成红色。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They used this mixture to tint their hair red.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Soap got its name, according to an ancient Roman legend, from Mount Sapo, where animals were sacrificed, leaving deposits of animal fat.
segments.zh: 根据一个古罗马传说，肥皂得名于萨波山，那里动物被献祭，留下动物脂肪的沉积物。雨水将这些沉积物连同木灰一起冲入台伯河沿岸的粘土中。妇女们发现这种混合物大大减少了洗衣服所需的力气。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Rain washed these deposits, along with wood ashes, down into the clay soil along the River Tiber.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Women found that this mixture greatly reduced the effort required to wash their clothes.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As Roman civilisation advanced, so did bathing.
segments.zh: 随着罗马文明的发展，沐浴也随之进步。第一座著名的罗马浴场建于公元前312年左右，由高架渠供水。浴场豪华，沐浴变得非常流行。到了公元2世纪，希腊医生盖伦推荐肥皂用于医疗和清洁目的。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The first of the famous Roman baths, supplied with water from their aqueducts, was built around 312 BC.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The baths were luxurious, and bathing became very popular.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And by the second century AD, the Greek physician Galen recommended soap for both medicinal and cleansing purposes.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: After the fall of Rome in 467 AD and the resulting decline in bathing habits, much of Europe felt the impact of filth on public health.
segments.zh: 公元467年罗马陷落后，沐浴习惯随之衰落，欧洲大部分地区感受到了污秽对公共卫生的影响。这种个人清洁的缺乏以及相关的不卫生生活条件是中世纪疾病爆发的主要因素，尤其是14世纪的黑死病。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This lack of personal cleanliness and related unsanitary living conditions were major factors in the outbreaks of disease in the Middle Ages, and especially the Black Death of the 14th century.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Nevertheless, soapmaking became an established craft in Europe, and associations of soapmakers guarded their trade secrets closely.
segments.zh: 尽管如此，制皂在欧洲成为一门成熟的手艺，肥皂制造商协会严密守护着他们的行业秘密。植物和动物油与植物灰烬一起使用，还加入了香水，这显然是第一次。逐渐地，更多种类的肥皂可用于剃须、洗发、沐浴和洗衣。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Vegetable and animal oils were used with ashes of plants, along with perfume, apparently for the first time.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Gradually more varieties of soap became available for shaving and shampooing, as well as bathing and laundering.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: A major step toward large-scale commercial soapmaking occurred in 1791, when a French chemist, Nicholas Leblanc, patented a process for turning salt into soda ash, or sodium carbonate.
segments.zh: 1791年，法国化学家尼古拉·勒布朗发明了一种将盐转化为纯碱（即碳酸钠）的工艺并取得专利，这标志着大规模商业化制皂迈出了重要一步。纯碱是从灰烬中获得的碱，能与脂肪结合形成肥皂。勒布朗工艺生产出了大量优质且廉价的纯碱。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Soda ash is the alkali obtained from ashes that combines with fat to form soap.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The Leblanc process yielded quantities of good-quality, inexpensive soda ash.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Modern soapmaking was born some 20 years later, in the early 19th century, with the discovery by Michel Eugène Chevreul, another French chemist, of the chemical nature and relationship of fats, glycerine and fatty acids.
segments.zh: 大约20年后，即19世纪初，另一位法国化学家米歇尔·欧仁·舍夫勒发现了脂肪、甘油和脂肪酸的化学性质及其相互关系，现代制皂业由此诞生。他的研究为脂肪化学和肥皂化学奠定了基础，制皂成为一门科学。19世纪的进一步发展使肥皂制造变得更加容易和廉价。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: His studies established the basis for both fat and soap chemistry, and soapmaking became a science.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Further developments during the 19th century made it easier and cheaper to manufacture soap.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Until the 19th century, soap was regarded as a luxury item, and was heavily taxed in several countries.
segments.zh: 直到19世纪，肥皂仍被视为奢侈品，在多个国家被课以重税。随着肥皂变得更容易获得，它成为日常必需品，而高额税收的取消进一步强化了这一趋势。肥皂从此成为普通人能够负担得起的物品，卫生标准也随之提高。随着肥皂的广泛使用，出现了更温和的沐浴皂以及用于洗衣机的肥皂——到20世纪初，消费者已经可以使用洗衣机了。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As it became more readily available, it became an everyday necessity, a development that was reinforced when the high tax was removed.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Soap was then something ordinary people could afford, and cleanliness standards improved.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: With this widespread use came the development of milder soaps for bathing and soaps for use in the washing machines that were available to consumers by the turn of the 20th century.

items.prompt: water was used to wash off 31 ________

pairs.note: 题干用'soap-like material'指代类似肥皂的物质，原文直接说'soap'，是同义替换。
题干和原文都出现'cylinders'，是原词复现，但题干加了数量'32'，原文没有，注意听数字。
题干'found in'与原文'discovered'是同义替换，都表示'发现于'。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'cylinders made of clay'，其中'clay'是材质，对应题干问的'soap-like material'所在的圆柱体材质，所以答案就是'clay'。

items.prompt: used a strigil – scraper made of 33 ________

pairs.p: metal instrument known as a strigil
pairs.note: 题干说strigil是某种材料做的刮刀，原文说strigil是金属工具，metal对应材料。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'with a metal instrument known as a strigil'，其中metal直接对应题干中strigil的制作材料，因此答案就是metal。

pairs.note: 原文用'tint'替换了题干'colour'，'this mixture'指代'soap'
原文直接出现'their hair'，与题干填空处对应

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'used this mixture to tint their hair red'，其中'this mixture'指代前文'soap'，'tint'对应'colour'，因此答案应为'hair'。

items.prompt: from about 312 BC, water carried to Roman 35 ________ by aqueducts

pairs.p: supplied with water from their aqueducts
pairs.note: 题干说水被运到某处，原文说澡堂由引水渠供水，意思相同。
时间完全对应，直接定位。
题干空格处应填地点，原文提到Roman baths，即罗马澡堂。

paraphrase.explain: 根据时间'around 312 BC'和'water from aqueducts'定位到原文第一句，发现'Roman baths'就是水被引去的地方。

items.prompt: decline in bathing contributed to occurrence of 36 ________

pairs.note: 原文加了'habits'，但意思完全一样。

pairs.p: major factors in the outbreaks of
pairs.note: 题干说'导致发生'，原文说'主要因素'，意思相同。
原文直接出现'disease'，答案就是它。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句第一句提到'decline in bathing habits'，与题干'decline in bathing'对应；第二句'outbreaks of disease'对应题干'occurrence of disease'，因此答案就是'disease'。

items.prompt: 37 ________ began to be added to soap

pairs.note: 原文用'for the first time'表示首次添加，对应题干'began to be added'
原文和题干都出现'soap'，直接对应

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'along with perfume, apparently for the first time'，其中'perfume'是答案，'for the first time'对应题干'began to be added'。

items.prompt: Leblanc invented a way of making soda ash from 38 ________

pairs.note: 题干'从...制造'对应原文'把...转化为'，意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到Leblanc发明了将盐转化为纯碱的工艺，题干问的是从什么原料制造纯碱，因此答案就是salt。

pairs.note: 'turned into' 和 'became' 都表示转变，是近义替换。
答案词 'science' 在原文中直接出现。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'soapmaking became a science'，其中 'became' 对应题干 'turned into'，'science' 是答案。

items.prompt: from 1800s, there was no longer a 40 ________ on soap

pairs.note: 题干说'不再有...'，原文说'高额税收被移除'，意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'high tax was removed'，对应题干'no longer a tax on soap'，所以答案填tax。
